description.text:
This entry represents the atypical serine/threonine kinase domain found in the RIO family members and similar proteins. RIO protein family are classified as atypical serine/threonine kinases and includes Rio1, Rio2 and Rio3, highly conserved from archaea to human [[cite:PUB00031542]]. They exhibit little sequence similarity with eukaryotic protein kinases, and hence, are classified as atypical protein kinases [[cite:PUB00033361], [cite:PUB00155945]]. This domain is also found at the C-terminal of EKC/KEOPS complex subunit BUD32 from Fungi and Kae1/Bud32 from archaea [[cite:PUB00059772]].

Rio1-3 have a distinct role in cytoplasmic maturation of the pre-40S pre-rRNA. Yeast Rio1 and Rio2 proteins are essential proteins that associate to ribosomal pre-40S particles and are involved in D-site cleavage in vivo [[cite:PUB00155945]]. They are also required for proper cell cycle progression and chromosome maintenance, and are necessary for survival of the cells. These proteins are involved in the processing of 20 S pre-rRNA via late 18 S rRNA processing.

PUB00031542.title: Crystal structure of A. fulgidus Rio2 defines a new family of serine protein kinases.

PUB00033361.title: A family portrait of the RIO kinases.

PUB00059772.title: Structure of the archaeal Kae1/Bud32 fusion protein MJ1130: a model for the eukaryotic EKC/KEOPS subcomplex.

PUB00155945.title: In vitro dimerization of human RIO2 kinase.

ec.description: ENZYME is a repository of information relative to the nomenclature of enzymes. It is primarily based on the recommendations of the Nomenclature Committee of the International Union of Biochemistry and Molecular Biology (IUBMB) and it describes each type of characterized enzyme for which an EC (Enzyme Commission) number has been provided.

representative_structure.name: Crystal Structure of A.fulgidus Rio1 serine protein kinase bound to ADP and Manganese ion
